new_classes.definition_en: Scotch whisky (distilled in Scotland, matured) with added soda water, marketed in bottles or other containers with an alcoholic strength of 40 % vol or more, classified under CN subheading 2208 30 61 (Chapter 22).
Quality representing the addition of soda water (carbonated water) to a spirit, used to discriminate CN subheading 2208 30 61 (whisky with added soda water) from other Scotch whisky subheadings.
Quality representing the geographic origin of distillation in Scotland, which is a defining characteristic of Scotch whisky classified under CN heading 2208 30.

coverage_explanation: The key distinguishing criteria from the legal text are captured: (1) the product is a whisky distilled from grain mash (subclass of Spirit), (2) it is Scotch (distilled and matured in Scotland, modelled via ScotlandDistillationOrigin quality), (3) soda water has been added (modelled via SodaWaterAddition quality and hasSodaWaterAddition property), and (4) the minimum alcoholic strength of 40 % vol is expressed as a decimalRange restriction. A small gap remains: the maturation requirement specific to Scotch whisky (aged in casks) is not explicitly modelled as an axiom, as no dedicated 'maturation process' class exists in the TBox yet; this is the main reason coverage is not 1.0. The grain-mash distillation basis could also be more precisely linked via hasMashBasis/GrainDistillation, but the parent class Spirit already implies distillation.
